name: Tomato Soup with Basil (Potage De Tomate a La Basilic)
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Fresh thyme leaves - OR - 1/4 tsp Dried thyme, crumbled
1 small Fennel bulb - trimmed and diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch tomatoes in a large pot of boiling water for 1 minute. Drain.
2. Rinse under cold water and drain again.
3. Peel the skin off the tomatoes using a small sharp knife.
4. Cut the peeled tomatoes into quarters and remove the seeds.
5. Puree the tomatoes in a blender or food processor.
6. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy large saucepan over medium-high heat.
7. Add the diced red bell pepper and saute until almost tender, about 3 minutes.
8. Stir in minced garlic and tomato paste; cook for 2 minutes.
9. Mix in the tomato puree, thyme (or dried thyme), and hot pepper sauce.
10. Remove from heat. Cover and let stand at room temperature for 2 hours.
11. Heat the remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy large skillet over medium heat.
12. Add fennel, zucchini, green bell pepper, mushrooms, and celery. Saute until crisp-tender, about 8 minutes.
13. Season with salt and pepper.
14. Bring the soup to a boil. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
15. Ladle the soup into bowls. Top with the vegetable mixture.
16. Garnish with basil leaves and serve.
